title: tar archive extraction has no path traversal protection
description: import.rs extract_archive: calls archive.unpack(dest) on a user-provided .tar.gz without setting set_unpack_xattrs(false) or checking for path traversal (../../../etc/cron.d/evil). The tar crate's unpack() does reject absolute paths by default, but relative paths containing .. are only blocked since tar 0.4.39. If using an older version, a crafted Mastodon archive could write files anywhere on the filesystem. Even with recent tar crate, the lack of explicit checks makes this fragile. Fix: iterate entries manually and validate each path component, or at minimum set archive.set_overwrite(false) and verify paths stay within dest after canonicalization.
